subject: a3f05aecaa3d550e2
findings: Delta round 2 (same reviewer, same worktree, HEAD e6d6833 verified one above ec51e8b, frozen files untouched). Both round-1 Majors closed with mechanisms and proven by my own fault injection. M-A: ci.yml absence branch now resets LASTEXITCODE to 0; my faithful GitHub-wrapper simulation gives ABSENT-STEP-EXIT=0 (was 128) and PRESENT 0; the new CiWrapperSimulation test extracts the REAL run text from ci.yml via ConvertFrom-Yaml (not a hand-copied mirror, throws if the step is renamed) and its red validity is proven (revert the fix, ABSENT test fails Expected 0 but got 128, restored byte-identical); the gating-matrix row honestly records the claim WAS false from C.2 until the fix, which is the record-keeping form we want, not over-engineering. M-B: header and usage example repointed to artifacts/reviews; line-18 historical incident kept accurate and annotated (honest annotation, not curation, correct call); a narrow runtime guard refuses -Out into docs/reviews via anchored path-segment regex, red-first tested 9 of 9, and my own probe confirms it allows the trickiest look-alike mydocs/reviews/x.md while refusing genuine segments including backslash and nested forms; the guard is sound scope, mechanizing R10's prose promise, not gold-plating. Rebase-list minors all 7 addressed; spot-verified CLAUDE.md:642, .gitattributes, worked-plan.md:23 all repoint to real moved files with moved-from annotations; git check-attr confirms -text still applies to verdicts at the new path (text unset, blobs pure LF) so it still serves the hashed-bytes need. The disclosed docs/artifacts dead pattern is confirmed never-existed and inert; recommend a one-line rebase deletion not an issue. Suites re-derived: tests 87/87, probes 12/12, Verify-Verdict 24/24 exit 0. ONE NEW MINOR from the M-A fix: CiWrapperSimulation calls ConvertFrom-Yaml unguarded and ci.yml installs only Pester not powershell-yaml; GitHub runners preinstall powershell-yaml so canonical CI passes and merge is not blocked, but it is an undeclared Law-7 substrate dependency inconsistent with the repo's Pester install-with-retry scar; rebase-list to install or guard it, and the CI handback must confirm the board-tooling step green on BOTH windows and ubuntu legs or this becomes blocking. Convergence clean: 2 Major to 0, no relocation, no swirl.
abstract: APPROVE-WITH-REBASE-LIST. Delta re-review of the same car's fix cycle at e6d6833. Both round-1 Majors are genuinely fixed with mechanisms, not patches, and I proved each non-vacuous by fault injection: M-A's CI checkpoint-fetch step now exits 0 on an absent branch (was 128) with a committed regression test that parses the real ci.yml and fails without the fix; M-B's verdict-landing tool is repointed to artifacts/reviews with its history honestly annotated and a narrow, tested runtime guard that refuses the retired docs/reviews path without false-positiving on look-alikes. All seven rebase-list minors are folded and three spot-verified against real moved files. Suites re-run: 87 tests, 12 probes, 24 verdicts, all green. Two non-blocking residuals: a pre-existing inert dead .gitattributes pattern (car-disclosed) and one new minor the M-A fix introduced, an undeclared powershell-yaml dependency in the regression test that GitHub runners satisfy but a stranger's clean box would not. Convergence is clean, 2 Majors to 0 with no findings relocated, so the branch proceeds to the whole-branch gate with the rebase list and a condition that the CI handback confirm the board-tooling step passes on both the windows and ubuntu legs. Read-only throughout; all fault injections reverted byte-identical; worktree clean; nothing committed or pushed.
